pattern: Every node can be the start of two candidate zigzag paths, one that opens by moving left and one that opens by moving right, and each of those only extends further if the child continues in the opposite direction.
hints: For each node, track the length of the best zigzag path that begins at that node by first moving left, and separately the best one that begins by moving right.
A path that opens by moving left can only continue growing through a move to the right at the next node, and vice versa, since the direction must alternate every step.
Update a running best-so-far as you visit every node, since the overall longest path can start anywhere in the tree, not just at the root.

edge_inputs.description: a path that zigzags right then left then right
a path that zigzags left then right then left then right
single node, the shortest possible tree
root with only a left child
root with only a right child
a pure left-only chain never alternates direction, so the longest zigzag stays short
a pure right-only chain never alternates direction either

stress.description: random general binary tree built by randomly filling open child slots
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 50000)))
    nodes = {0: [1, None, None]}
    open_slots = [(0, 'left'), (0, 'right')]
    next_id = 1
    for _ in range(1, n):
        idx = rng.randrange(len(open_slots))
        parent_id, side = open_slots.pop(idx)
        nid = next_id
        next_id += 1
        nodes[nid] = [1, None, None]
        if side == 'left':
            nodes[parent_id][1] = nid
        else:
            nodes[parent_id][2] = nid
        open_slots.append((nid, 'left'))
        open_slots.append((nid, 'right'))
    from collections import deque
    out = []
    q = deque([0])
    while q:
        cur = q.popleft()
        if cur is None:
            out.append(None)
            continue
        val, l, r = nodes[cur]
        out.append(val)
        q.append(l)
        q.append(r)
    while out and out[-1] is None:
        out.pop()
    return [out]
